top_signals.title: The United States produced more crude oil than any other country in 2025
top_signals.summary: The United States remained the world's largest crude oil producer in 2025, according to our International Energy Statistics database, extending a streak that began in 2018 when the United States overtook Russia to become the world's leading producer.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: MOLLE, PALS, and the Empty Loops of America’s Civil-Military Divide
top_signals.summary:
Editor’s note: This is the third article in a limited series celebrating American defense technologies born from wartime and their effects on broader national security, politics, and society. This series will run for several weeks to commemorate America’s 250th anniversary, and winners will be selected by a reader vote undertaken through our newsletter later this summer. Prior installments can be found at the Arsenal of Innovation page. In the half-century since the Gates Commission established the all-volunteer force — and especially over the last 25 years — the relationship between the American soldier and the state has changed profoundly. top_signals.title: We built a vulnerability vending machine: AI tokens in, zero-days out
top_signals.summary: Intruder built an AI-powered "vulnerability vending machine" that combines code slicing with LLMs to automatically discover complex software vulnerabilities. The company explains how the system found and exploited a previously unknown WordPress plugin zero-day, with additional discoveries already under responsible disclosure. [...]

top_signals.title: PRA and FCA propose new captive insurance regime to drive UK growth and competitiveness
top_signals.summary: Innovative new proposals aim to establish the UK as a centre for the fast-growing captive insurance market.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Growth and regulation - speech by Andrew Bailey
top_signals.summary: Given at The Financial and Professional Services Dinner, Mansion House
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: U.S. exports of crude oil and petroleum products reached record in April
top_signals.summary: U.S. petroleum exports reached a record in April as disruptions to international crude oil and refined product flows through the Strait of Hormuz increased global demand for U.S. exports. Exports increased to 13.6 million barrels per day (b/d) in April, 15% more than the previous record set in March.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Stanford study says 5-minute Bitcoin prediction markets enable settlement manipulation
top_signals.summary: Researchers found that Polymarket’s five-minute Bitcoin prediction markets create incentives to manipulate spot prices around contract settlement, proposing longer settlement windows as a potential fix.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Zoom warns of critical account takeover vulnerability
top_signals.summary: Zoom is warning of a critical vulnerability in its desktop client and software development kit for Windows that could be exploited by an unauthenticated party to hijack accounts. [...]

top_signals.title: United earnings top estimates but airline expects $6 billion in added fuel costs
top_signals.summary: United reported higher revenue for premium, corporate and no-frills basic economy tickets and higher revenue for both domestic and international trips.

top_signals.title: The 250-year history of U.S. energy consumption
top_signals.summary: Over the 250-year history of our nation, energy consumption has evolved from wood use in the 18th and 19th centuries to today's use of modern renewable, hydrocarbon, and nuclear technology. In 2025, total energy used in the United States was 96 quadrillion British thermal units (quads), up 2% from 2024, but below 2007's record 99 quads. Petroleum was the most-used energy source last year, followed closely by natural gas. Use of renewable, coal, and nuclear energy each made up about 9% of total energy use.
